title: External Flow (II) VWAP
subtitle: VWAP-Based Directional Signal — Price vs. Volume-Weighted Average Price
author: Christian Benjamin (lynnchris)
verdict: EXTRACT — VWAP formula, S/R derivation method, and confirmation interval pattern are all directly usable.

series_evolution.part_9: Bridge architecture established. Signal = last_close vs 10-day average
series_evolution.part_10: Same bridge, but upgrades the signal from naive average to VWAP. Also adds support/resistance levels as output — the first part in the series to derive S/R programmatically from data rather than drawing lines manually.

core_concept.name: VWAP as Dynamic S/R and Directional Filter
core_concept.description: VWAP is not just an average — it represents the TRUE fair value of the instrument weighted by trading volume. Price above VWAP = buyers paid above fair value = bullish. Price below VWAP = sellers dominated = bearish. VWAP also acts as a magnet — price tends to revert to it.
core_concept.vwap_formula: VWAP = sum(typical_price × volume) / sum(volume)  where typical_price = (H+L+C)/3
buy_conditions: current close > VWAP (price trading above fair value)
sell_conditions: current close < VWAP (price trading below fair value)
core_concept.neutral: current close ≈ VWAP (equilibrium — no directional bias)
core_concept.relevance_to_creviaderiv: VWAP is the clean version of what Part 9 tried to do with a simple average. We use ATR-normalized velocity (VWV) instead of VWAP, but the principle is the same: weight price movement by volume participation. Our VWV is strictly superior for intraday signals on volatile instruments.

num_candles.description: Bars to collect for VWAP calculation

confirmation_interval.description: Signal must persist N periods before alerting

support_resistance_derivation.note: Simple but effective. Major = absolute range. Minor = recent average. Two-tier S/R hierarchy.

steps.name: collect_h1_bars
steps.description: MQL5 collects 150 H1 bars (timestamp, H, L, O, C, V), filters zeros, formats as CSV

steps.name: python_vwap_calc
steps.description: Python: parse CSV → Pandas → calculate VWAP, typical_price, major/minor S/R

steps.name: signal_evaluation
steps.description: Compare last close to last VWAP value

steps.name: confirmation_check
steps.description: Signal must appear for confirmation_interval consecutive periods before alerting

components_to_build.name: VWAPCalculator
components_to_build.note: Returns VWAP series. Caller takes last value for current VWAP level. Vectorised with pandas cumsum.

components_to_build.name: SupportResistanceDeriver
components_to_build.contract: derive_sr(highs, lows, window) -> {major_sup, major_res, minor_sup, minor_res}
components_to_build.note: Major S/R from full window min/max. Minor S/R from rolling N-period average of lows/highs.

keep: VWAP formula: typical_price=(H+L+C)/3, cumsum(price*vol)/cumsum(vol) — industry standard, correct
Two-tier S/R: major (absolute min/max) + minor (rolling average) — useful hierarchy
Signal confirmation interval — same concept as our departure candle check
JSON response with S/R levels — more informative than just signal direction
150-bar window for calculation — enough data to establish meaningful VWAP
discard: Using VWAP alone as signal — insufficient for production (no zone, no structure context)
Minor S/R as 3-bar rolling average — too short, noisy. Use our zone-based S/R instead.
Major S/R as absolute window min/max — too mechanical. Our zone-based S/R is superior.
improve: Replace raw VWAP signal with zone-based entry (VWAP can inform zone quality but not replace it)
Replace minor S/R (3-bar rolling average) with our ZoneIdentifier output
Add VWAP as a FILTER not a signal: price > VWAP → allow BUY setups only
Use our VWV (velocity-weighted volume) instead of raw VWAP — normalized for ATR, handles synthetics
Add session-anchored VWAP: reset VWAP at session open (London, NY) not on lookback window
Add VWAP standard deviation bands (VWAP ± 1SD, ±2SD) as dynamic S/R zones

creviaderiv_insights.vwap_vs_vwv: VWAP measures where price traded on average weighted by volume. Our VWV measures HOW MUCH velocity each bar has weighted by volume. VWAP=WHERE (fair value), VWV=HOW FAST (energy). Both are needed. VWAP as directional filter + VWV as energy gate = superior to either alone.
creviaderiv_insights.session_anchored_vwap: The article uses a rolling 150-bar VWAP but the industry standard is session-anchored VWAP (reset at London or NY open). Session-anchored VWAP is far more meaningful as a fair value reference for intraday trading.
creviaderiv_insights.two_tier_sr_validation: Major S/R (window min/max) + Minor S/R (recent rolling) is correct thinking. Our system: major = zone from ZoneIdentifier (supply/demand), minor = current bar H/L from previous session. More structurally meaningful than a rolling 3-bar average.

article_figures.title: VWAP Formula Visualization
article_figures.insight: Shows VWAP formula and its relationship to price. Confirms typical_price = (H+L+C)/3 as the standard.

article_figures.title: VWAP Strategy Chart Example
article_figures.insight: Price chart with VWAP line. Shows price oscillating around VWAP and using it as dynamic S/R.

article_figures.title: System Flowchart
article_figures.insight: MT5 → CSV → Flask → VWAP calc → JSON → MT5. Same bridge architecture as Part 9.

article_figures.title: WebRequest Settings GIF
article_figures.insight: Shows MT5 settings needed to allow WebRequest. Confirms the bridge setup complexity — another reason to be Python-native.

article_figures.title: Test Results
article_figures.insight: Shows signal on chart with VWAP level, entry point, and S/R levels displayed.

article_figures.title: Market Response Validation
article_figures.insight: Multiple signals aligned with VWAP. Confirms price respects VWAP as S/R in liquid markets.
